what: Battle cut-in famous-quote (名台詞) bank: the banner text shown when a pilot uses an ID command in combat. 942 records, each addressed by a 943-entry u32 offset table inside the code binary (so records may grow; this bank grew when the quotes were re-encoded at full length).
framing: record = header + body + terminator 00 03 00 01 + zero padding to a 4-byte boundary. header = 00 05 <quote-set id u16le> (a few records were re-authored in the headerless 00 04 continuation form; their original JP header is kept as jp_header). body = text lines separated by {00}; a line's leading {03} commits the previous line and advances the banner one page, {04} continues without a page commit; the same byte values mid-line are the punctuation 。 and ·. The cut-in renderer draws the whole record (it does not stop at {00}), and the cartridge streaming codec expands {F0:n} dictionary macros — record framing must therefore be preserved exactly.
